title: Sphagneticola calendulacea
taxonomy: Sphagneticola calendulacea is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to the tropical regions of the Americas and is also known as Wedelia or Creeping Oscar. The plant's scientific classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Sphagneticola, Species: S. calendulacea. Sphagneticola calendulacea is a member of the Compositae family, which is an older name for the Asteraceae family. The plant is a part of the Aster family, which is known for its wide variety of flowering plants.
uses: Sphagneticola calendulacea, also known as the wedding bush, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its roots and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can aid in the treatment of wounds and infections. In some parts of the world, the plant is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential in reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall health. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of Sphagneticola calendulacea for medicinal use.
